author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

title: Chapter XIII. 36–38.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: While the Lord Jesus was commending to the disciples that holy love wherewith they should love one another, “Simon Peter saith unto Him, Lord, whither goest Thou?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So, at all events, said the disciple to his Master, the servant to his Lord, as one who was prepared to follow.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as for the same reason the Lord, who read in his mind the purpose of such a question, made him this reply: “Whither I go, thou canst not follow me now;” as if He said, In reference to the object of thy asking, thou canst not now.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He does not say, Thou canst not; but “Thou canst not now.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He intimated delay, without depriving of hope; and that same hope, which He took not away, but rather bestowed, in His next words He confirmed, by proceeding to say, “Thou shalt follow me afterwards.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why such haste, Peter?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Rock (petra) has not yet solidified thee by His Spirit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Be not lifted up with presumption, “Thou canst not now;” be not cast now into despair, “Thou shalt follow afterwards.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what does he say to this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Why cannot I follow Thee now?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I will lay down my life for Thy sake.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saw what was the kind of desire in his mind; but what the measure of his strength, he saw not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The weak man boasted of his willingness, but the Physician had an eye on the state of his health; the one promised, the Other foreknew: the ignorant was bold; He that foreknew all, condescended to teach.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How much had Peter taken upon himself, by looking only at what he wished, and having no knowledge of what he was able!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How much had he taken upon himself, that, when the Lord had come to lay down His life for His friends, and so for him also, he should have the assurance to offer to do the same for the Lord; and while as yet Christ’s life was not laid down for himself, he should promise to lay down his own life for Christ!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus” therefore “answered him, Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wilt thou do for me what I have not yet done for thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Wilt thou lay down thy life for my sake?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Canst thou go before, who art unable to follow?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why dost thou presume so far? what dost thou think of thyself? what dost thou imagine thyself to be?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear what thou art: “Verily, verily, I say unto thee, The cock shall not crow, till thou hast denied me thrice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See, that is how thou wilt speedily become manifest to thyself, who art now talking so loftily, and knowest not that thou art but a child.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou promisest me thy death, and thou wilt deny me thy life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou, who now thinkest thyself able to die for me, learn to live first for thyself; for in fearing the death of thy flesh, thou wilt occasion the death of thy soul.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as much as it is life to confess Christ, it is death to deny Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or was it that the Apostle Peter, as some with a perverse kind of favor strive to excuse him, did not deny Christ, because, when questioned by the maid, he replied that he did not know the man, as the other evangelists more expressly affirm?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As if, indeed, he that denies the man Christ does not deny Christ; and so denies Him in respect of what He became on our account, that the nature He had given us might not be lost.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whoever, therefore, acknowledges Christ as God, and disowns Him as man, Christ died not for him; for as man it was that Christ died.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who disowns Christ as man, finds no reconciliation to God by the Mediator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For there is one God, and one Mediator between God and men, the man Christ Jesus.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that denies Christ as man is not justified: for as by the disobedience of one man, many were made sinners; so also by the obedience of one man shall many be made righteous.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He that denies Christ as man, shall not rise again into the resurrection of life; for by man is death, and by man is also the resurrection of the dead: for as in Adam all die, even so in Christ shall all be made alive.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And by what means is He the Head of the Church, but by His manhood, because the Word was made flesh, that is, God, the Only-begotten of God the Father, became man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how then can one be in the body of Christ who denies the man Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or how can one be a member who disowns the Head?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But why linger over a multitude of reasons when the Lord Himself undoes all the windings of human argumentation?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He says not, The cock shall not crow till thou hast denied the man; or, as He was wont to speak in His more familiar condescension with men, The cock shall not crow till thou hast thrice denied the Son of man; but He says, “till thou hast denied me thrice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is that “me,” but just what He was, and what was He but Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whatever of Him, therefore, he denied, he denied Himself, he denied the Christ, he denied the Lord his God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Thomas also, his fellow-disciple, when he exclaimed, “My Lord and my God,” did not handle the Word, but only His flesh; and laid not his inquisitive hands on the incorporeal nature of God, but on His human body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so he touched the man, and yet recognized his God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, what the latter touched, Peter denied; what the latter invoked, Peter offended.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The cock shall not crow till thou hast denied me thrice.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Although thou say, “I know not the man;” although thou say, “Man, I know not what thou sayest;” although thou say, “I am not one of His disciples;” thou wilt be denying me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, which it were sinful to doubt, Christ so spake, and foretold the truth, then doubtless Peter denied Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not accuse Christ in defending Peter.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let infirmity acknowledge its sin; for there is no falsehood in the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When Peter’s infirmity acknowledged its sin, his acknowledgment was full; and the greatness of the evil he had committed in denying Christ, he showed by his tears.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He himself reproves his defenders, and for their conviction, brings his tears forward as witnesses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nor have we, on our part, in so speaking, any delight in accusing the first of the apostles; but in looking on him, we ought to take home the lesson to ourselves, that no man should place his confidence in human strength.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else had our Teacher and Saviour in view, but to show us, by making the first of the apostles himself an example, that no one ought in any way to presume of himself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And that, therefore, really took place in Peter’s soul, for which he gave cause in his body.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet he did not go before in the Lord’s behalf, as he rashly presumed, but did so otherwise than he reckoned.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For before the death and resurrection of the Lord, he both died when he denied, and returned to life when he wept; but he died, because he himself had been proud in his presumption, and he lived again, because that Other had looked on him with kindness.
